title: Enrollment Failed
description: Detects when the Autopilot enrollment has explicitly failed.
explanation:
The Autopilot enrollment has explicitly failed.

**Key facts extracted from this session's events:**

- **Failure reason:** `{{reason}}`
- **First failed app:** `{{appName}}`
- **ESP failed subcategory:** `{{failedSubcategory}}`
- **ESP HRESULT:** `{{errorCode}}`

When an ESP Apps-subcategory failure is paired with HRESULT `0x87D1041C`, the device-side install most likely completed but the Intune detection rule did not match afterwards — see the **App Detection Failure During ESP** rule (ANALYZE-APP-013) in this same analysis for the actionable root cause and remediation steps. Any field still shown as a literal `{{…}}` placeholder was simply not recorded for this session — for example, a non-ESP failure path has no ESP failed-subcategory or HRESULT.

remediation.title: Investigate enrollment failure
steps: Start with the more specific ANALYZE-APP-* / ANALYZE-ESP-* rules that fired alongside this one — they correlate specific HRESULTs to actionable causes (e.g. 0x87D1041C → detection rule mismatch in ANALYZE-APP-013)
Read the `reason` field on the enrollment_failed event for the immediate trigger (e.g. esp_terminal_failure, hello_timeout, max_lifetime)
If a HRESULT is present, look it up against the ESP error catalog (Microsoft Learn: Autopilot ESP error codes)
Review other analyze rule results for correlated issues (disk space, network, app failures)
Check the device event timeline for the sequence of events leading to failure
Verify the Autopilot profile and ESP configuration in Intune
